page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX-512F, AVX-512 VNNI, and AES instructions. The instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is relatively weak, as shown by bottom-25% scores in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside poor single-threaded compression and decompression metrics. However, the 24 dedicated physical cores deliver average multi-core performance. Multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests all fall within the middle 50% of benchmarked systems. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate average performance, though the overall Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

Given its hardware profile and performance characteristics, the ecs.g5.6xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that benefit from parallel processing rather than high single-thread performance. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis database operations, and moderate LLM inference tasks using smaller models. The absence of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on external block storage. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable multi-core execution for enterprise applications without the premium of high-frequency compute-optimized or memory-optimized hardware classes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal weak single-core capabilities, with single-threaded compression and Geekbench scores in the lower percentiles. Conversely, its multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average tier, supported by the 24 physical cores and AVX-512 instruction sets. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that do not require high single-thread speeds. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.6xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 24 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 96 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, offering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated multi-core general-purpose platform offering balanced memory-to-CPU resources for parallelized enterprise workloads and static web serving.

96.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core performance suitable for parallel processing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for static web serving, Redis, and multi-threaded applications
